headline: PayPay (PAYP) Valuation Check After Nasdaq Debut Visa Partnership And Index Inclusion
summary: PayPay (PAYP) has quickly moved into the spotlight after its Nasdaq debut, where shares traded above the IPO price and the company raised about US$880 million, attracting attention from global institutional investors. See our latest analysis for PayPay. Since listing, PayPay has seen brisk trading, with a 1 day share price return of 16.41% on Friday to US$21.14 and a 32.12% year to date share price return. Index inclusion, Visa partnership news and talk of a possible Tokyo dual listing have...

headline: PayPay Nasdaq Debut And Visa Deal Raise Valuation Questions For Investors
summary: PayPay, trading on Nasdaq as NasdaqGS:PAYP, completed a successful IPO on Nasdaq, marking what has been described as the largest U.S. listing by a Japanese fintech company in a decade. The offering attracted major international investors, including Abu Dhabi Investment Authority, Qatar Investment Authority, and Visa. Alongside the IPO, PayPay announced a partnership with Visa to explore entry into the U.S. market. Reports also indicate the company may consider a future dual listing on the...

headline: Why Mastercard and Visa Are the Definition of Forever Stocks
summary: Despite a weak year for financials, Visa and Mastercard remain forever stocks due to their global duopoly, massive profit margins, and forecast industry growth.

headline: Circle Bank Charter And Earnings Put USDC Business Model In Focus
summary: Circle Internet Group (NYSE:CRCL) has received preliminary approval to form a national trust bank, giving its USDC stablecoin business a new regulatory footing. The company also reported strong quarterly earnings, supported by rising USDC adoption and new partnerships with Visa and BlackRock. Circle Internet Group is appearing on more investor radars, with NYSE:CRCL closing at $115.38 and posting a 99.4% return over the past 30 days. The stock is also up 13.2% over the past week and 38.2%...
